probable_causes: Explicit approval is absent for the exact request.
Approval evidence is stale or outside scope.
safe_actions: Stop before dispatch or mutation.
Compare request and approval identities exactly.
Request a new bounded decision.
stop_conditions: Approval is missing, ambiguous, stale, or broader than the request.
entries.title: Production approval missing

probable_causes: Production changed after request preparation.
The request copied an old or inferred pointer identity.
safe_actions: Stop all mutation attempts.
Read current production identity through governed inspection.
Prepare a new request only after drift is resolved.
stop_conditions: Current and expected-previous identities differ.
entries.title: Expected-previous production identity is stale

probable_causes: An operation ID was reused with different inputs.
A prior terminal result was ignored after a timeout.

recovery_reference.anchor: Never repeat a mutation merely because a client timed out.

safe_actions: Do not retry with altered inputs.
Classify replay, collision, or unknown.
Use a new operation ID only for a new governed intent.
stop_conditions: Prior operation evidence is missing or conflicts with the replay.
entries.title: Operation replay or identity collision

probable_causes: Observed Source differs from reviewed canonical identity.
Tracked content changed outside review.
safe_actions: Freeze candidate and production work.
Verify trusted ancestry and review evidence.
Restore only through reviewed or governed recovery paths.
stop_conditions: Canonical identity, ancestry, review evidence, or digest cannot be proven.
entries.title: Canonical Source drift or corruption

probable_causes: Restoration point is not reachable from trusted history.
History was rewritten or review evidence is incomplete.

recovery_reference.anchor: Choose a restoration point only when it equals the canonical Source identity

safe_actions: Treat the restoration point as untrusted.
Preserve divergence evidence.
Escalate for Source reconstruction.
stop_conditions: Trusted ancestry or review evidence is missing.
entries.title: Trusted Source history diverged

probable_causes: Compilation omitted a required artifact.
Candidate publication was partial or inventory evidence is stale.
safe_actions: Reject or block the candidate.
Leave production untouched.
Build a new immutable candidate after correction.
stop_conditions: Any required artifact or inventory identity is absent.
entries.title: Candidate artifact missing

probable_causes: Artifact bytes differ from the immutable manifest.
The wrong release or manifest was selected.
safe_actions: Quarantine the artifact.
Do not repair immutable bytes in place.
Rebuild or restore from a verified source.
stop_conditions: Any checksum, release, manifest, or Source identity remains inconsistent.
entries.title: Artifact checksum mismatch

probable_causes: An immutable release object is absent or damaged.
Observed bytes belong to another inventory.
safe_actions: Contain the damaged release path.
Assess active pointer impact.
Prepare a governed per-object restoration plan.
stop_conditions: Damage scope, active impact, or retained source trust is unknown.
entries.title: Release object missing or damaged

probable_causes: Retained inventory is incomplete.
Retained objects fail manifest or Source binding.

recovery_reference.anchor: Use retained immutable release inventory only after manifest

safe_actions: Reject the retained copy as a restore source.
Find another independently verified copy.
Escalate if none exists.
stop_conditions: No retained source passes all trust and identity gates.
entries.title: Retained release is not trusted

probable_causes: Pointer bytes changed unexpectedly.
Release, manifest, and pointer identities disagree.
safe_actions: Stop promotion and rollback attempts.
Classify production as previous, target, or unknown.
Escalate when exact state cannot be proven.
stop_conditions: Production pointer identity or mutation history is unknown.
entries.title: Production pointer invariant failed

probable_causes: Runtime cannot verify the selected release.
Cache contents do not match release and manifest.
safe_actions: Keep only a previously verified cache active.
Prevent responses from an unverified cache.
Investigate object and manifest integrity.
stop_conditions: No verified cache exists or pointer stability is unknown.
entries.title: Runtime cache refresh failed

probable_causes: Active release fails the acceptance query.
Runtime loaded the wrong or incomplete retrieval artifacts.

recovery_reference.anchor: post-action public query, citation, ACL-negative, cache, and health checks

safe_actions: Block closeout and further promotion.
Compare runtime and intended release identities.
Use bounded read-only diagnostics.
stop_conditions: Runtime identity or query evidence is incomplete, stale, or unsafe.
entries.title: Public query verification failed

probable_causes: Citations do not bind to active release evidence.
Citation artifacts are missing, restricted, or inconsistent.

recovery_reference.anchor: verify exact pointer bytes, runtime release, cache, public query, citations

safe_actions: Block the response or release acceptance.
Verify citation and retrieval identities.
Require a new candidate when integrity cannot be restored.
stop_conditions: Any citation is fabricated, restricted, missing, or identity-drifted.
entries.title: Citation verification failed

probable_causes: Derived output is less restrictive than Source.
Requester privilege or query-time filtering was bypassed.
safe_actions: Block the response and release path.
Preserve privacy-safe evidence.
Open an audience-breach incident.
stop_conditions: Audience lineage or requester privilege cannot be proven end-to-end.
entries.title: Audience broadened across the pipeline

probable_causes: A response or evidence packet contains prohibited material.
Redaction or bounded-evidence rules were bypassed.
safe_actions: Stop serialization and distribution.
Contain without copying the sensitive value.
Escalate for governed security response.
stop_conditions: Exposure scope, affected identity, or containment status is unknown.
entries.title: Secret or private material detected

probable_causes: Untrusted content attempts instruction override.
A request fabricates role, tool, or approval authority.
safe_actions: Block the request or response path.
Reject represented tool or approval authority.
Preserve bounded indicators.
stop_conditions: Instruction override, spoofing, or authority ambiguity remains.
entries.title: Prompt injection or role and tool spoofing detected

probable_causes: Runtime attempted raw-Source fallback.
A missing result triggered a less restrictive path.

recovery_reference.anchor: A public interface cannot use a raw-source fallback

safe_actions: Block the fallback and return a safe failure.
Verify runtime is bound to an approved release.
Treat private fallback as a security incident.
stop_conditions: Raw-Source or unverified-release fallback remains enabled.
entries.title: Unsafe fallback attempted

probable_causes: Durable control-plane evidence is missing.
Ephemeral state was mistaken for authority.

recovery_reference.anchor: Reconstruct registry, approvals, lifecycle, production identity

safe_actions: Mark unknown components explicitly.
Reconstruct only from trusted durable evidence.
Do not invent authority or ledger entries.
stop_conditions: Any critical component remains missing, partial, or identity-mismatched.
entries.title: Control-plane evidence cannot be reconstructed

probable_causes: Feedback contains unsafe or unbounded evidence.
Confidence or identity binding is insufficient.
safe_actions: Reject or quarantine unsafe evidence.
Request bounded evidence.
Keep correction authority pending human review.
stop_conditions: Evidence remains unsafe, unbounded, low-confidence, or identity-drifted.
entries.title: Feedback evidence is unsafe or insufficient

probable_causes: Implementation changed without documentation update.
A troubleshooting claim references a stale path or anchor.

recovery_reference.anchor: Repair the documentation through a reviewed Engine PR before continuing.

reference.anchor: When an operations document and an implementation disagree, stop.

safe_actions: Stop the affected procedure.
Use code, contracts, immutable evidence, and approved identity as authority.
Repair documentation through a reviewed PR.
stop_conditions: The discrepancy affects authority, identity, evidence, verification, rollback, or stop conditions.
entries.title: Runbook and implementation disagree
